title: Hartogia lancifolia
intro: Hartogia lancifolia is a plant species native to tropical Africa, belonging to the family Rutaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its reported anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Hartogia lancifolia include limonoids, flavonoids, and essential oils, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Hartogia lancifolia include decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which are used to treat a range of health issues, particularly those related to the skin and respiratory system.
